title: Nursery Rhyme. CXXXII. Songs.
content:
[This is the version generally given in nursery collections, but is somewhat different in the 'Pills to Purge Melancholy,' 1719, vol. iv, p. 148.]

        One misty moisty morning
        When cloudy was the weather,
        There I met an old man
        Clothed all in leather;
        Clothed all in leather,
        With cap under his chin, - 
        How do you do, and how do you do,
        And how do you do again!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Unknown, titled "Nursery Rhyme. CXXXII. Songs.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
